Nahash had been an enemy to Israel, but showed kindness to David. Therefore, David resolved gratefully to return it. If someone gives alms out of pride, though God will not reward it, whoever receives the alms should give thanks for them. Those who have ill-will toward their neighbors are determined not to believe that their neighbors have any good-will toward them. There is nothing so well meant that it may not be misinterpreted, and often is, by people who love no one but themselves. The best people must not think it strange if they are misrepresented in this way. Charity does not suspect evil. According to the customs of those days and countries, Hanun treated David's ambassadors with great contempt. David showed much concern for his servants. Let us learn not to take unjust accusations to heart; they will disappear and bring shame only to those who say or do them; while a reputation that has been wrongly harmed will grow again in a little time, just as these beards did. God will bring forth your righteousness like light, so wait patiently for him.

Those who are at war with the Son of David not only give provocation but begin the war. God has power to send against those who defy his wrath, which will convince them that no one ever hardened their heart against God and prospered. Christ’s soldiers should strengthen each other's hands in their spiritual warfare. Let nothing be lacking in us, whatever the outcome may be. When we are careful to do our duty, we can leave the result with God, assuredly hoping for his salvation in his own way and time.

Here is a new attempt by the Syrians. Even a defeated cause will persist as long as there is life; this is what the enemies of the Son of David do. But now the promise made to Abraham and repeated to Joshua, that the borders of Israel would extend to the river Euphrates, was fulfilled. Learn from this that it is dangerous to help those who have God against them; for when they fall, their helpers will fall with them.
